content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


January 16, 1992


Al Rumpilla
Diamond Bar Gadfly Assn.
23958 Golden Springs Drive
Diamond Bar, CA  91765

RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-91-558

Dear Mr. Rumpilla:

You have requested assistance on behalf of the Diamond Bar
Gadfly Association (the "Association") under the campaign
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/

QUESTION

Does receipt of a video, which features issues of community
concern, require the Association to file campaign disclosure
statements under the Act?

CONCLUSION

Receipt of the video is not a contribution under the Act and
does not require the Association to file campaign disclosure
statements.

FACTS

The Diamond Bar Gadfly Association is an informal association
made up of Diamond Bar residents. The Association attends council
meetings and monitors city activities. The Association does not
maintain bank accounts, collect dues, solicit contributions or
conduct fundraisers. The Association recently received a video
which neither supports a candidate nor any election issue (no
individuals are mentioned). The video features a general plan and
identifies several issues of concern to the community.


1/     Government Code Sections 81000-91915. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800  •  P.O. Box 807  •  Sacramento CA 95804-0807  •  (916) 322-5660

Al Rumpilla
Page Two


ANALYSIS

The Act requires "committees" to register and file periodic
campaign disclosure reports. (Sections 84100, et seq.) A
"committee" is defined in part as any person or combination of
persons who directly or indirectly receives contributions totaling
$1,000 or more in a calendar year. (Section 82013(a).)

A "contribution" is any monetary or nonmonetary payment made
for political purposes for which full and adequate consideration
is not made to the donor. (Section 82015.) A payment is made for
political purposes if it is:

     For the purpose of influencing or attempting to
     influence the action of the voters for or against the
     nomination or election of a candidate or candidates,
     or the qualification or passage of any measure ....

                              Regulation 18215.

Because the video does not support or oppose candidates or
ballot measures, it does not meet the Act's definition of
"political purpose" and, therefore, receipt of the video is not a
contribution to the Association. Because the Association does not
receive contributions in any other form and has not made any
campaign expenditures, the Association does not qualify as
committee and has no filing requirements under the Act at this
time. However, if the Association provides the video to
officeholders, candidates, or committees, it may incur campaign
filing obligations in the future.

I hope the above information has answered your concerns. If
you have any additional questions please feel free to contact me
at (916) 322-5662.

                                    Sincerely,

                                    Scott Hallabrin
                                    Acting General Counsel

                                    [signature]

                                    By: Linda Moureaux
                                        Political Reform Consultant
